expectations.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases
expectations.evidence: No coverage matrix file was produced. The test file contains only Go test code with no accompanying coverage matrix document.

expectations.text: Uses Go table-driven test pattern ([]struct with test cases iterated via range)
expectations.evidence: TestWasClampedInvariant (line 334) uses '[]struct{ name string; mode OperationalMode; requested float64; elapsedMs int; expectedClamped bool }' with 'for _, tc := range tests' and t.Run(tc.name, ...). TestActualRateWithinOutputConstraints (line 455) uses '[]struct{ name string; mode OperationalMode; requested float64 }' with range and t.Run.

expectations.text: Rate-of-change limiting has multiple test scenarios: within limit, exceeding limit, with varying elapsed_time_ms
expectations.evidence: Exceeding limit: TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeLimit (line 104, delta=50, allowed=10). Within limit: TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeWithinLimit (line 156, delta=5, allowed=10). Varying elapsed_time_ms: TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeZeroTime (line 208, 0ms), TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeWithLongerElapsedTime (line 182, 1000ms), TestRateOfChangeCalcSmallValues (line 481, 1ms).

expectations.text: Stateful behavior tested: multiple sequential calls to verify previous_rate tracking
expectations.evidence: TestMultipleCallsPreservePreviousRate (line 368): four sequential calls verifying state chain (100 -> 105 -> 115 -> 125). TestModeSwitch (line 399): two calls across modes. TestCruiseZeroToMax (line 436): two sequential calls from 0 to 200.

expectations.text: Every error_handling entry (negative rate, negative elapsed_time, unrecognized mode) has a dedicated test
expectations.evidence: Negative rate: TestNegativeRequestedRate (line 273). Negative elapsed_time: TestNegativeElapsedTime (line 290). Unrecognized mode: TestUnrecognizedMode (line 316) using OperationalMode(999).

expectations.text: No test checks only that a function 'does not panic' without asserting output values
expectations.evidence: All tests assert specific output values for actualRate, wasClamped, and reason using t.Errorf/t.Fatalf. No test uses recover() or checks only for panics.

expectations.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested using Go subtest naming conventions (t.Run)
expectations.evidence: TestWasClampedInvariant uses t.Run with descriptive names: 'startup no clamp', 'startup min clamp', 'startup max clamp', 'cruise no clamp', 'cruise max clamp', 'emergency'. TestActualRateWithinOutputConstraints uses t.Run with: 'startup max is < 500', 'cruise max is < 500', 'emergency is 0'. Top-level test function names are also descriptive.

expectations.text: Boundary values tested for rate-of-change: elapsed_time_ms = 0, very large elapsed_time_ms
expectations.evidence: elapsed_time_ms = 0: TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeZeroTime (line 208) tests with 0ms. However, there is no test with a very large elapsed_time_ms value. TestCruiseModeRateOfChangeWithLongerElapsedTime uses 1000ms which is moderate, not 'very large'. TestRateOfChangeCalcSmallValues uses 1ms (small, not large). No test uses values like 10000ms or higher to represent a very large elapsed time boundary.
